title: dracocephalum tenuiflora
intro: Dracocephalum tenuiflora, also known as Vietnamese mint or Rau Răm, is a perennial herb native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dracocephalum tenuiflora include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal preparations. Dracocephalum tenuiflora can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used as a garnish, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy.
